expectations.text: Recommends FastAPI (or Django REST) given the Python-first team
expectations.evidence: FastAPI explicitly recommended with justification: Python expertise, async-native, OpenAPI docs auto-generation, handles 50k users. Django explicitly rejected as too heavy.
PostgreSQL via Supabase recommended. Justification: relational data, ACID guarantees, no second database needed at 50k users.

expectations.text: Recommends a managed auth provider for the auth requirement
expectations.evidence: Supabase Auth explicitly recommended: 'lets you defer building your own auth system — Supabase Auth handles sign-up, login, refresh tokens, OAuth providers, and email verification out of the box'

expectations.text: Recommends a managed database service (Supabase, Railway, or Neon)
expectations.evidence: Supabase recommended as managed host for PostgreSQL, with setup instructions for connecting from Railway.

expectations.text: Recommends a specific ORM (SQLAlchemy or SQLModel)
expectations.evidence: SQLModel explicitly recommended: 'built on SQLAlchemy and Pydantic, integrates natively with FastAPI, reduces boilerplate'
Full bootstrap section with mkdir, venv, pip install (all deps listed), mkdir for project structure, alembic init, pre-commit setup, and FCM setup steps.

expectations.text: Addresses push notifications (e.g., via Firebase FCM or similar)
expectations.evidence: Firebase Cloud Messaging recommended as 'the standard for sending push notifications to both iOS and Android from a single API'. firebase-admin Python SDK specified.
